data.title: Nestle India Shares Experience Volume Surge in Today's Session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Shares of Nestle India experienced a notable surge in trading volume during today's session, accompanied by a price increase of 0.40%. The stock is currently trading at Rs 2,347.70, reflecting heightened market activity.

The increased volume and price movement have drawn attention to Nestle India, a prominent constituent of key indices including the NIFTY 50 and NIFTY 100. This signifies the company's broad presence and influence within the Indian stock market.

Financial Performance


Nestle India's financial performance provides context to its current market position. Recent quarterly and annual results indicate consistent revenue and profit generation.

Quarterly Highlights:


  • Mar 2025: Revenue reached Rs 5,503.88 Cr, an increase from Rs 4,779.73 Cr in Dec 2024.
  • Net Profit: Stood at Rs 885.88 Cr for the quarter, compared to Rs 695.82 Cr in the previous quarter.
  • EPS: Reported at Rs 9.06.

Annual Highlights:


  • Revenue: Decreased from Rs 24,393.89 Cr in 2024 to Rs 20,201.56 Cr in 2025.
  • Net Profit: Decreased from Rs 3,932.84 Cr in 2024 to Rs 3,231.54 Cr in 2025.
  • EPS: Decreased from Rs 40.79 in 2024 to Rs 33.27 in 2025.

Balance Sheet Snapshot


Key figures from the company's balance sheet provide additional insight:
  • Share Capital: Maintained consistently at Rs 96 Cr.
  • Reserves & Surplus: Increased from Rs 3,244 Cr to Rs 3,913 Cr.
  • Total Liabilities: Increased from Rs 10,523 Cr to Rs 12,193 Cr.
  • Total Assets: Increased from Rs 10,523 Cr to Rs 12,193 Cr.

Key Financial Ratios


Nestle India's financial ratios reflect its efficiency and stability:
  • P/E Ratio: Valued at 67.65 in Mar 2025 compared to 64.29 in Mar 2024.
  • P/B Ratio: Valued at 54.12 in Mar 2025 compared to 75.71 in Mar 2024.
  • Debt to Equity: Increased from 0.01 to 0.19.

The latest revenue figures show Rs 10,098.12 Cr in March 2024, Rs 4,813.95 Cr in June 2024, Rs 5,104.00 Cr in September 2024, Rs 4,779.73 Cr in December 2024 and Rs 5,503.88 Cr in March 2025. The net profit was Rs 1,670.81 Cr, Rs 746.60 Cr, Rs 903.21 Cr, Rs 695.82 Cr and Rs 885.88 Cr for the same period respectively. The EPS was Rs 17.33, Rs 7.74, Rs 9.33, Rs 7.14 and Rs 9.06 respectively for the same period.

The neutral sentiment analysis from Moneycontrol as of 2025-05-02 suggests a balanced outlook on the stock.

With the stock currently trading at Rs 2,347.70, Nestle India's shares reflect heightened market activity.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: DLF shares decline by 3.05%
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Shares of DLF experienced a downturn, falling by 3.05% to Rs 659.15 during today's trading session. This movement positions DLF as a notable mover within the market, drawing attention from investors and analysts.

DLF is included in the NIFTY NEXT 50 and NIFTY 100 indices. The company's performance is often viewed in relation to these broader market indicators, providing context for its stock movements.

Financial Performance


Recent financial data provides a snapshot of DLF's financial health. Here’s an overview of the company's consolidated financial performance:

Quarterly Performance:


  • December 2023: Revenue was reported at Rs 1,521.25 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 376.50 Cr and an EPS of Rs 2.65.
  • March 2024: Revenue increased to Rs 2,134.84 Cr, accompanied by a net profit of Rs 630.11 Cr and an EPS of Rs 3.72.
  • June 2024: Revenue stood at Rs 1,362.35 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 339.28 Cr and an EPS of Rs 2.61.
  • September 2024: Revenue rose to Rs 1,975.02 Cr, and the net profit significantly increased to Rs 1,043.41 Cr, resulting in an EPS of Rs 5.58.
  • December 2024: Revenue was reported at Rs 1,528.71 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 440.40 Cr and an EPS of Rs 4.28.

Annual Performance:


  • 2020: Revenue was Rs 6,082.77 Cr, with a net loss of Rs -1,479.21 Cr and an EPS of Rs -2.41.
  • 2021: Revenue decreased to Rs 5,414.06 Cr, but the company turned profitable with a net profit of Rs 477.29 Cr and an EPS of Rs 4.42.
  • 2022: Revenue slightly increased to Rs 5,717.39 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 843.62 Cr and an EPS of Rs 6.06.
  • 2023: Revenue remained relatively stable at Rs 5,694.83 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,100.92 Cr and an EPS of Rs 8.22.
  • 2024: Revenue increased to Rs 6,427.00 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 1,630.42 Cr and an EPS of Rs 11.02.

Financial Ratios


Key financial ratios provide additional insights into DLF's performance:
  • Basic EPS (Mar 2024): Rs 11.02
  • Book Value/Share (Mar 2024): Rs 159.30
  • Dividend/Share (Mar 2024): Rs 5.00
  • Face Value: Rs 2

Profitability and Efficiency:


  • Gross Profit Margin (Mar 2024): 41.30%
  • Operating Margin (Mar 2024): 39.00%
  • Net Profit Margin (Mar 2024): 25.36%

Return Ratios:


  • Return on Equity (Mar 2024): 6.91%
  • ROCE (Mar 2024): 5.43%
  • Return on Assets (Mar 2024): 4.52%

Liquidity and Leverage:


  • Current Ratio (Mar 2024): 2.30
  • Debt to Equity: 0.12
  • Interest Coverage Ratio: 7.45

Valuation:

  • P/E (Mar 2024): 81.40
  • P/B (Mar 2024): 5.64

With the stock's current price at Rs 659.15, DLF's stock movement reflects broader market dynamics and investor reactions to the company's financial standing.

data.title: Nifty, Sensex sink into red as volatility spikes amid geopolitical uncertainties, weekly expiry
data.description:
Broader markets and sectors like auto, FMCG, and banking led the decline.

After a range-bound session, Nifty 50 and Sensex saw deepening losses in trade on Thursday, May 8, as investors offloaded their equity holdings amid rising geopolitical tensions, while uncertainty spiked as a result of the weekly expiry session.

At 14:51 pm, the Sensex was down 253.83 points or 0.31 percent at 80,492.95, and the Nifty was down 108.20 points or 0.44 percent at 24,306.20. About 1610 shares advanced, 1776 shares declined, and 123 shares were unchanged.

The broader markets saw sharper cuts, with the Nifty Smallcap 100 and the Nifty Midcap 100 indices tumbling up to 1.5 percent.

On the sectoral front, the indices painted a mixed picture. Auto, FMCG, banking and pharma stocks were among the top losers, with the indices slipping nearly two percent. On the flip side, IT and media stocks managed to eke out some gains, despite a turbulent session.

The fear gauge, India VIX, that measures market volatility through options trading soared 11 percent to 21.16, as uncertainty dictated the market sentiment. Further, the weekly index expiry also led to volatility spiking.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

Further, the Indian Armed Forces this morning targeted air defence radars and systems at multiple locations inside Pakistan. One of the significant targets — an air defence system positioned in Lahore — was successfully neutralised, according to a press release by PIB.

With markets currently in a wait-and-watch mode due to rising geopolitical tensions, Jefferies analysts cautioned that a further escalation along the borders cannot be ruled out.

“Any potential escalation would be negative for tourism, high-beta stocks, and industrials in particular. However, any consequent market correction would likely be short-lived,” they said in their India strategy note, where they have reduced weightage on tourism and real estate sectors in their model portfolio.

Devarsh Vakil, Head of Prime Research, HDFC Securities said, "The 24,300 level, where substantial Put options have been written, is anticipated to be strong support. Conversely, a decisive move by the Nifty above the 24,500 resistance level could trigger sharp short covering."

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl
